title: Bulk report - Week 34
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market endured a notably weaker week overall, with the BCI 5TC shedding more than $4,000 before closing at $23,160, aided by a $742 uptick today. The Pacific struggled from the outset, with the lack of consistent miner presence weighing on sentiment and pushing C5 rates below $9.00 by midweek. The week, however, closed on a slightly firmer note, with two miners active and a fixture concluded at $9.50, offering some encouragement.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market endured a notably weaker week overall, with the BCI 5TC shedding more than $4,000 before closing at $23,160, aided by a $742 uptick today. The Pacific struggled from the outset, with the lack of consistent miner presence weighing on sentiment and pushing C5 rates below $9.00 by midweek.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The excitement this week predominantly emanated from the Atlantic again. The continued lack of early tonnage in the North had a profound effect on rates, with both robust fronthaul and sound transatlantic demand rates lurched, with $30,000 reported on a super-spec 87,000-dwt type delivery Continent for a US East Coast to India run the headline rate on fronthaul trades, whilst some talk that close to $17,000 timecharter equivalent was achieved in the North Atlantic for a transatlantic voyage deal, adding further fuel to the fire.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: Despite many still being away on their summer vacations, it was a solid week for the sector. Demand remained in the US Gulf for the transatlantic runs, with an ultramax being heard fixed at around $30,000 for the same.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: Like the larger size, it was generally a positive week for the Handy sector, certainly from the Atlantic. Better levels of enquiry were seen from both the North and South Atlantic.
